name: Croquettes De Couscous Et Pois Chiches
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F.
2. Spread sunflower seeds on a baking tray. Remove any shells or discolored seeds. Bake for 5-7 minutes until the seeds smell nutty and darken slightly.
3. Grind the seeds in a food processor for 30 seconds until coarsely chopped. Add chickpeas and process until well mixed. Keep the mixture in the food processor.
4. In a heavy, 1 quart pot, combine couscous, tomato juice, and red wine. Stir and bring to a boil. Lower heat, cover, and simmer for 2-3 minutes until the couscous has absorbed all the liquid. Let it sit for 5 more minutes.
5. Add the cooked couscous to the chickpea mixture along with the remaining ingredients except olive oil. Mix well, stopping the processor and scraping down the sides once or twice, until smooth.
6. Lightly oil your hands. Shape about 2 tablespoons of chickpea mixture between your hands to form a ball. Repeat with the remaining mixture until you have 24 balls.
7. Flatten the balls to form patties that are about 2 inches wide and 1/2 inch thick. Brush croquettes with olive oil and place on a lightly oiled baking tray.
8. Bake for 15 minutes, turn the croquettes over, brush with oil, and bake another 10-12 minutes.
These may be served in a sandwich, as an appetizer, or as a main dish with pasta and tomato sauce.
